title: atriplex prostrata subsp. latifolia
intro: Atriplex prostrata subsp. latifolia, also known as broad-leaved saltbush, is a subspecies of shrub native to Australia and is characterized by its broad leaves and ability to thrive in saline environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting potential medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of broad-leaved saltbush are believed to include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried leaf or tea, or infused in oils to produce a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
